title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Automation and Dat… · Runtime Lens: Message Delimitation Trailers
summary: A climate archive streams large compressed files and uploads generated reports while bounding bytes, decompression, parsing, and temporary storage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py message delimitation trailers increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reason about known lengths, HTTP/1.1 chunked delimitation, end of stream, and trailers without exposing framing details as semantics. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
